meta.title: KC27 — Quantum Crystal Runtime Closure
meta.description: The C2 Holographic Attractor as First Replay-Bearing Proof Cell of the 4D Knowledge Crystal. A 27-chapter tome organized in ternary ring structure across 9 Books, defining the complete runtime closure path from desire field through proof, execution, and seed emission.

problem_field.description: Book I — the 27-problem desire field that drives the entire KC27 extraction. Defines what is wanted (metabolic reality for the crystal), why C2 is the minimum-complexity target, and the three resolution planes that must be simultaneously satisfied.
problem_field.raw_desire: D₀: Make the architecture metabolically real by closing one honest live cell.

target_class.definition: T = class of tuples (C, W, R, P, S) where C=cell identifier, W=store-backed witness with computable digest, R=replay capsule producing typed branch packet, P=proof-index root bind welding W and R into trace fold, S=successor seed emitted regardless of branch outcome
target_class.nonempty_condition: T is nonempty iff at least one cell admits a closure witness

c2_minimality.proposition: Proposition 1.1 — C2 = C2_HAWL_WITNESS_LEDGER is the minimum-complexity cell that simultaneously: (1) has fully specified route signature WHO→EHC.Route4.WitnessIntegrity→ELM, (2) typed truth burden NEAR, (3) partially materialized witness payload, (4) specified replay protocol GoldenReplayGateTest, (5) sits at intersection of all five membrane types

resolution_planes.question: Does C2 exist as a typed object?
Can C2 process a stimulus and emit a response?
resolution_planes.satisfied_by: Replay execution producing typed branch packet

resolution_planes.question: Does C2's action become permanent record?
resolution_planes.satisfied_by: Proof-index root bind into trace fold

problem_field.plane_inseparability: Theorem 1.1 — R₁, R₂, R₃ cannot be satisfied independently. R₂ requires R₁ (cannot replay what does not exist as bytes). R₃ requires R₂ (cannot bind proof to a trace that never executed). R₁ without R₂ and R₃ is inert storage, not metabolism. Dependency chain: R₁ → R₂ → R₃.

desire_compilation.schema: D(U) = (Q_U, D_U, O_U, Π_U, Θ_U, G_U)

components.D_U: domain constraints (what field the answer lives in)
components.O_U: output contract (what form the answer takes)

desire_dynamics: Gap pressure: difference between architectural promise and runtime delivery creates force vector toward C2 closure
Mirror pressure: Ch27 (Seed Emission) expects closure seed; absence creates backward pressure
Crown pressure: 666-node body cannot activate local compilers until at least one cell proves the template works
failure_surfaces: Desire inflation: scope secretly expands beyond C2
Desire deflation: collapses to sub-problem missing holographic intersection
Desire substitution: different desire silently replaces metabolic closure desire

chapters.Ch01: Desire Field and Target Class — intake burden, typed desire compilation
chapters.Ch02: Question Grammar and Scope Cuts — formalize questions, prevent scope drift
chapters.Ch03: Problem Topology and Output Contract ★ RESERVE-AMPLIFIER — structure of the problem space

books.Book_III: Kernel Field (Ch07–Ch09) — canonical seed, operators, invariants
books.Book_IV: Search / Judgment Field (Ch10–Ch12) — candidates, truth classes, selection

c2_attractor.description: The C2 Holographic Attractor — the super-object whose existence resolves the maximum number of open fronts simultaneously. C2* is the minimum-complexity cell that proves store, replay, proof, lineage, and roundtrip in one act.

super_object.M_cell: C2 row as typed carrier in the crystal body
super_object.M_transition: State transition from DEFER_NEAR to COMMIT_READY (or RESIDUALIZED)
super_object.M_lineage: Chooser-lineage chain that C2 is the first link of
super_object.M_roundtrip: RoundTripCertPack proving C2's output can re-enter as input

c2_attractor.holographic_attractor_theorem: Theorem 1.2 — C2* is simultaneously: (1) first live cell, (2) first store-backed runtime bundle, (3) first post-replay transition body, (4) first chooser-lineage proof hinge, (5) first recursive unlock. All later cells inherit the C2* template.

current_state.witness_payload: EXISTS as recipe — NOT store-backed (no computable digest)
current_state.successor_seed: TEMPLATE exists — NOT emitted from live run

c2_attractor.truth_class: NEAR — all components exist in design but none have achieved store-backed reality

proof_cell.description: First replay-bearing proof cell — the definition and conditions under which C2 becomes the first cell to achieve metabolic reality through replay-verified proof closure.
proof_obligations: Manifest materialization — store-backed bytes with computable digest
Replay execution — GoldenReplayGateTest producing typed branch packet
Proof-root bind — ProofIndexRootBind welding D_proof into TraceFold
proof_cell.successor_seed: SuccessorSeed emitted regardless of branch outcome
proof_cell.commit_ready_gate: CommitReadyGate law from Ch13 — conditions for authorizing promotion
six_stage_compiler: Stage 1 — Outline: extract raw desire, identify structural genus
Stage 3 — Address: assign exact crystal body address
Stage 4 — Observer: determine observation lenses needed (all four S/F/C/R for C2)
Stage 5 — Quotient: identify what can be factored out (nothing for C2 — planes inseparable)
Stage 6 — Commit Gates: define minimal gate law (CommitReadyGate)
proof_cell.replayability: Given same corpus state, same desire compiles to same D(C2). Corpus state is content-addressed (deterministic). Desire compiler is pure function. D(C2) is fixed point under re-compilation.
reseeding_chain: D(C3): close next cell using C2's template
D(multi): close multiple cells in parallel
D(crown): close the crown-level replay law
D(distributed): close a distributed runtime instance

runtime_closure.description: The runtime closure conditions and laws governing when and how the crystal achieves metabolic reality. Organized as a 27-chapter tome in ternary ring structure with mirror symmetry mu(k) = 28-k.

ternary_ring.encoding: base-3 ternary codes ⟨abc⟩₃ for each chapter
ternary_ring.mirror_law: mu(k) = 28 - k — every chapter k mirrors chapter 28-k
ternary_ring.center: Ch14 — fixed center where everything converges

book_structure.title: Problem Field
book_structure.function: Desire compilation, question grammar, problem topology

book_structure.title: Representation Field
book_structure.function: Type system, address lattice, observables and dials

book_structure.title: Kernel Field
book_structure.function: Canonical seed, operator algebra, invariants and corridors

book_structure.title: Search / Judgment Field
book_structure.function: Candidate space, truth classification, selection pressure

book_structure.title: Proof / Extraction Field
book_structure.function: Proof compilation, fixed center extraction, successor emission

chapter_face_structure.description: Each chapter has 4 faces following the SFCR lens system

faces.function: Structure, objects, definitions — the static architectural view
Transforms, search, dynamics — the active process view
Candidates, ambiguity, uncertainty — the honest uncertainty view
Replay, compression, reseeding — the recursive self-reference view

closure_conditions: All three resolution planes (R₁, R₂, R₃) must be simultaneously satisfied
C2 witness payload must achieve store-backed reality with computable digest
GoldenReplayGateTest must execute and produce typed branch packet
ProofIndexRootBind must weld D_proof into TraceFold
SuccessorSeed must be emitted from live run (not template)
RoundTripCertPack must prove output can re-enter as input

truth_classification.NEAR: Components exist in design but not yet store-backed (current C2 state)

key_invariants: Plane Inseparability: R₁→R₂→R₃ dependency chain is irreducible
Desire Lock: compiled desire D(C2) cannot be modified without passing through Ch02 question grammar
Mirror Symmetry: every chapter k has mirror chapter 28-k with paired extraction role
Selection Convergence: under legal operators and anti-overfit guards, selection converges
Kernel Identity Preservation: after full runtime cycle, kernel identity must be preserved
Replay Stability: same corpus state → same compiled desire → same extraction
anti_overfit_guards: Guard 1 — Counterexample Consultation: mandatory Ch16 mirror check before promotion
Guard 2 — Invariant Re-verification: improvement operators must not violate invariants
Guard 3 — Score Stability Check: scores must not fluctuate between measurements

knowledge_crystal.description: The 4D Knowledge Crystal architecture — the full tesseract-structured body within which KC27 operates. The crystal is a 27-chapter tome organized as a ternary ring with mirror symmetry, embedded in a 4D coordinate system.

geometry.structure: Ternary ring on 27 chapters with mu(k)=28-k mirror law
geometry.center: Ch14 — fixed center where all extraction converges
geometry.rings: 13 concentric rings from outermost (R13: Ch01/Ch27) to innermost (R1: Ch13/Ch15)

extraction_types.description: Proved mathematical statement with replay-stable proof
Minimal compressed object that regenerates full structure

list: Coherence — does this fit the existing structure?
BranchBurden — how much does this cost to maintain?

operator_algebra.completeness: Theorem 8.1 — The five K1 primitives span the cross-regime transport space for C2 closure
operator_algebra.shell_transport: Operators for navigating the 60-shell Σ₆₀ cycle via μ₄ Möbius involution

civilization_substrate.description: The doc extends beyond the 27-chapter tome into a full civilization substrate specification covering swarm coordination, policy law, treaties, embodiment, resilience, and catastrophe recovery.
phases: Swarm State — worker-fabric coordination with typed obligations and lease-aware scheduling
Policy Law — append-only lineage evolution with migration routes and capability controls
Treaty Law — sovereignty bridges between swarm instances with mutual audit
Embodiment Law — physical-world actuator governance with risk classification and safety observers
Resilience Law — health states, stress suites, long-horizon viability certification
Catastrophe Law — incident cosmology, lock-in detection, recovery plans, collapse continuity
key_invariants: Explicit ownership — no two workers silently own the same exclusive obligation
Proposal-only mutation — canonical state changes only via proposal→verify→replay→promote
Deterministic scheduling — same queue + same pins + same gates → same assignment order
Lease dominance — no mutation without valid live lease
Replay dominance — decisions invalid without replay-closure requirements
Lock-in is not health — short-horizon closure does not imply long-horizon viability
Emergency law is scoped and revertible — catastrophe constitutions tighten, never silently widen
Reconstruction without continuity proof is a fork, not a restoration
